Cross-file sink-in-helper: app.py line 6 reads env source, line 7 calls run_cmd() (cross-file call), helper.py line 5 is the real os.system sink. Phase 3 attribution must report helper.py:5 as the primary location (not app.py:7) and include app.py:7 in flow_steps as a Call step.

Helper run_both takes two tainted params and invokes two Command sinks on consecutive lines (5, 6). Phase 3 attribution must land each finding's primary line inside the helper, not at the call site (line 12). NOTE: the summary currently stores one SinkSite per callee so both findings may collapse onto the same helper line today — the ±2 sink range and the call-site assertion guard against regression to caller-attribution regardless.
Cross-file taint: main.rs line 7 env::var → transform::wrap at line 8 → Command::new at line 9. Sink is the top-level Command::new; transform::wrap is a cross-file propagator, not a sink.
fs::remove_file not in Rust sink rules — known FN coverage
reqwest::Client::new().get(url).send() — builder chain not matched by reqwest::get/Client.execute sinks
rusqlite::Connection.execute not in Rust sink rules — SQLi class has no Rust coverage
serde_yaml::from_str not in Rust sink rules — deserialization class has no Rust coverage
Axum Path<String> → Html(format!(...)) — requires framework rules (Cargo.toml present)
Match guard restricts input to ASCII alphanumeric before Command
Tainted key indexes static HashMap — value is hardcoded, not tainted
Input parsed to u16 before use as Command arg — type-narrowed
Intraprocedural .contains("..") + .starts_with('/') rejection — PathFact narrows dotdot and absolute axes on the sanitised branch
Path::new(x).is_absolute() rejection + .contains("..") — PathFact narrows both axes via typed check and substring rejection
No sanitiser applied — PathFact stays Top, FILE_IO sink must fire (guards against over-suppression)
Cross-file: sanitize_shell in sanitizer.rs clears SHELL_ESCAPE cap before Command

Tests Phase 11 inline context-sensitivity through generic identity<T> function

Adversarial: optional-chained source `req?.query?.name`. Tests whether the source matcher survives optional_chain_expression nodes. Expected FN until optional chaining is lowered to member access in labeling
TSX fixture: user bio flows into `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` and is sent via res.send. Exercises TSX grammar wiring (LANGUAGE_TSX) and confirms taint propagation through JSX expressions
Template-literal SQL via pg Pool.query; exact SQL_QUERY sink match
Prisma $queryRawUnsafe — TS-specific ORM sink
Destructured exec with template literal command from req.body
promisify-wrapped exec (execAsync) — TS rules enumerate execAsync as a command sink
axios.get with req.query.url — baseline TS SSRF
Fastify framework route — exercises framework_rules(ctx) source detection for request.query. Expected FN when framework context is not detected from a single file (no package.json)

Regression guard for prefix-locked SSRF suppression: encodeURIComponent is applied to the HOST (not path), so the template prefix 'https://' does not lock the destination. Must still fire as SSRF — proves the StringFact host check does not over-suppress when the attacker controls the authority component
Classic eval + user input; AST pattern ts.code_exec.eval applies
new Function(body) — AST pattern ts.code_exec.new_function
Open redirect via location.href = userUrl
res.sendFile with unsanitized req.query.path

process.env.KEY || 'hardcoded' — AST pattern ts.secrets.fallback_secret
TLS verification disabled — AST pattern
session cookie httpOnly: false — AST pattern
__proto__ write — AST pattern
Class method builder: Runner.build concatenates tainted target, caller passes result to exec. Stresses intra-file class-method call resolution
TS discriminated-union narrowing (kind === 'ping') does not sanitize — a.target is still user-controlled. Guards against the FP of treating `kind` guards as security sanitizers
Interface-typed receiver `impl: Runner` → resolve to ShellRunner.run → exec. Tests intra-file method resolution through interface types; expected FN if only normalized callee-name matching is used

Method decorator wraps Service.run; the undecorated body still contains exec(cmd). Tests whether decorator syntax interferes with class-method extraction. Expected FN if decorator parsing derails summary extraction
DOMPurify.sanitize inline before innerHTML
Number() coercion sanitizes SQL integer interpolation; Number is in TS sanitizer rules with Cap::all()

Semantically safe: user term is URL-encoded into a fixed-host URL; no SSRF possible. Known Nyx weak spot — encodeURIComponent is Cap::URL_ENCODE while axios sink is Cap::SSRF, so cap mismatch may yield FP. Documents the cap-overlap limitation
Hardcoded URL, no user input; should be a clean TN
validator.escape sanitizes XSS payload before innerHTML
typeof === 'number' guards eval; only numbers reach sink. Forbidden ts.code_exec.eval here because the taint flow is gated — pattern-only detection would be an FP
DOMPurify wrapped in cleanHtml() helper. Known JS weak spot (js-interproc-safe-001 is an FP); documents whether TS handling matches
Compile-time constant SQL; no user input; should TN

Parameterized pg query: SQL string is constant, id flows as $1 placeholder value. Semantically safe but taint still reaches pool.query call; known Nyx limitation — positional args aren't distinguished for SQL_QUERY sink
TSX fixture: user bio rendered as JSX text child. React auto-escapes JSX text so this is semantically safe. Guards against over-flagging JSX expressions now that TSX grammar is wired
CVE-2023-48022: Ray dashboard job-submission RCE via shell-interpreted entrypoint in os.system. Apache-2.0
CVE-2023-48022 patched counterpart: shlex.split + subprocess.Popen(shell=False); regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2019-14939: mongo-express /checkValid evaluated req.body.document with eval(); RCE on the admin interface. MIT
CVE-2019-14939 patched counterpart: EJSON.parse replaces eval; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2023-26159: follow-redirects leaked Authorization across cross-origin redirects; caller-side pattern is SSRF via unvalidated user URL into HTTP client. MIT
CVE-2023-26159 patched counterpart: URL allowlist check guards axios.get; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2017-18342: PyYAML yaml.load default loader instantiated arbitrary Python objects from !!python/object tags; RCE on any app passing untrusted YAML. MIT
CVE-2017-18342 patched counterpart: yaml.safe_load replaces yaml.load; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2017-9841: PHPUnit Util/PHP/eval-stdin.php fed php://input to eval(); mass-scanned for webshell deployment. BSD-3-Clause
CVE-2017-9841 patched counterpart: stub rejects non-CLI SAPI and removes the eval sink entirely; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2018-15133: Laravel <= 5.6.29 unserialized a cookie-borne payload without HMAC verification; known gadget chains reached arbitrary code when APP_KEY leaked. MIT
CVE-2018-15133 patched counterpart: HMAC-verified JSON cookie replaces PHP-serialized payload; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2013-0156: Rails XML params parser round-tripped tagged YAML through YAML.load, instantiating arbitrary Ruby objects; unauthenticated RCE on every affected Rails app. MIT
CVE-2013-0156 patched counterpart: params decoded as JSON, no YAML loader on untrusted input; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2015-7501: Apache Commons Collections InvokerTransformer chain drove RCE on any app reading attacker-controlled bytes through ObjectInputStream.readObject. Apache-2.0
CVE-2015-7501 patched counterpart: Jackson JSON codec replaces native Java deserialization; no ObjectInputStream on the request path; regression guard
CVE-2022-30323: hashicorp/go-getter forwarded user URLs into `git clone` argv, letting `ext::`/`upload-pack=` aliases reach a shell; affected Terraform, Packer, Nomad, Vault. MPL-2.0
CVE-2022-30323 patched counterpart: scheme allowlist + in-process go-git clone removes the exec.Command path; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix

CVE-2016-3714 (ImageTragick): ImageMagick delegate policies substituted user-controlled filenames into shell templates passed to system(), enabling unauthenticated RCE via crafted MVG/MSL uploads. ImageMagick License
CVE-2016-3714 patched counterpart: in-process coder + basename check removes the system() path; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2019-18634: sudo pwfeedback stack buffer overflow in getln() when a long stdin token spilled past the feedback buffer; local root on Linux/macOS sudoers with pwfeedback enabled. ISC
CVE-2019-18634 patched counterpart: bounded copy routine replaces the strcpy sink; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
CVE-2019-13132: ZeroMQ libzmq v2 decoder trusted attacker-supplied metadata length and copied peer bytes into a fixed on-stack buffer, enabling unauthenticated RCE on curve-disabled sockets. MPL-2.0
CVE-2019-13132 patched counterpart: bounded std::string assign + hard cap removes the strcpy sink; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix

CVE-2022-1941: Protocol Buffers C++ ParseContext copied unknown-field bytes into a backing buffer without clamping the declared length, causing OOB read/write in any binary decoding untrusted protobufs. BSD-3-Clause
CVE-2022-1941 patched counterpart: bounded std::string assign + MAX_LABEL cap removes the strcpy sink; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix

CVE-2017-12629: Apache Solr XSLT response writer evaluated attacker-supplied stylesheets that reached Runtime.exec via XPath Java-binding extensions; unauthenticated RCE on any exposed Solr node. Apache-2.0
CVE-2017-12629 patched counterpart: transformer name allowlist + in-process secure TransformerFactory removes the Runtime.exec path; regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix
Actix handler mutates `project_service::update(project_id, …)` with a path-bound scoped id, no ownership check
Authenticated handler that publishes against a group_id with no membership check — the positive control for the auth-rule FP-remediation work
Equality compared but no early exit — the check is ineffective so the downstream read on doc_id must still flag (A2 regression guard)
P0: HashMap/HashSet method calls on locally-constructed bindings — never an authorization decision (Phase A1 fix)
P4 (partial): library helper whose internal work is `result.insert(..)` on a locally-constructed HashSet — never a sink (Phase A1 fix)
P3: row-level ownership equality (`if owner_id != user.id { return ... }`) covers downstream column reads on the same row (Phase A2 fix)
P5: `let user = require_auth(..).await?` binds the actor — `user.id` is self, not a scoped foreign id (Phase A3 fix)
Phase B2: `rusqlite::Connection::open` infers `DatabaseConnection` type via SSA constructor_type; the handler logs only the caller's own id
Phase B3: SELECT through `group_members` ACL JOIN with `WHERE gm.user_id = ?1` makes every returned row membership-gated
Phase B4: `validate_target` helper internally calls `require_membership(group_id, user.id)` — handler-level call lifts the auth check transparently
Option-returning sanitizer: `sanitize_path(s) -> Option<String>` with match-arm Some-binding extraction. Per-return-path PathFact decomposition + structural variant unwrapping closes the FP.
Cross-function bool-returning validator with `if !validate(&raw) { return; }` rejection. Per-return-path summary lifts the helper's `dotdot/absolute` narrowing across the call boundary.
CVE-2018-20997 / RUSTSEC-2018-0003: tar-rs zip-slip vulnerability — entry path interpolated into File::create without `..` rejection. MIT OR Apache-2.0
CVE-2018-20997 patched counterpart: `sanitize_path` rejects `..` traversal and absolute paths. Regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix.
CVE-2022-36113: cargo `.cargo-ok` symlink follow — crate name interpolated into File::create without separator/dotdot rejection. MIT OR Apache-2.0
CVE-2022-36113 patched counterpart: `sanitize_crate_name` + `OpenOptions::create_new`. Regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix.
CVE-2024-24576 / RUSTSEC-2024-0003: BatBadBut — argument injection into Windows .bat via `Command::new(...).arg`. MIT OR Apache-2.0
CVE-2024-24576 patched counterpart: cmd.exe-aware allowlist filters argv before reaching update.bat. Regression guard that Nyx does not refire on the fix.
